threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Overground' instead of just jumping on a train. Real locals know it’s just part of the Tube family now.
Caught a late 133 last night; the driver sang along to classic reggae, and even the usual rowdy bunch quieted down. Magic moment on a dreary route.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a street performer juggling flaming torches in front of a kebab shop at 2 AM, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you catch a bus-turned-restaurant, serving currywurst at midnight while you wait for a late tram. Pure city magic!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can always spot a local by their resigned eye-roll when BART's delayed yet again. Tourists still have hope it'll be on time.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a bus today that stopped mid-ride because the driver had to break up a fight between two regulars over a stolen backpack. Just another day.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the Gaudí hype; they miss the cozy corners like Plaça de la Vila and the quirky local shops that make it home.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they ask for 'tapas' like it's an appetizer, while the locals just order 'comida' and sit back.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think you can hop on the Tube without checking for strikes—good luck with that!
Last night on the 47, a guy started singing opera. By the time we hit London Bridge, half the bus was clapping. Only in Bermondsey, I tell you.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a kid playing in the street, dodging the coming tram while balancing an ice cream cone perfectly—only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, it's normal to find people selling homemade vegan cakes at bus stops between the trains. Only here do baked goods become part of the public transport experience.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their ability to navigate BART delays while still managing to get a burrito from La Taqueria before it closes.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading Muni riders with a ukulele by the 4th Street station. Some clapped, others just plugged in. That's SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for nightlife, but many locals avoid the rambles of Travessera. The real charm is in the quiet plazas during the day.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newcomers; they still take photos of the ‘famous’ La Padrera as if it’s not just another building on Passeig de Gràcia.
